backup_purpose: Phase 6.7 - Pattern ID Migration to BVD Standard

vulnerability_patterns.name: Reentrancy Attack
vulnerability_patterns.description: External call followed by state change allows attacker to re-enter contract and manipulate state
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Use checks-effects-interactions pattern, reentrancy guards, or pull payment pattern
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use ReentrancyGuard from OpenZeppelin", "Move state changes before external calls", "Use .transfer() instead of .call{value}()"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Function makes external call to untrusted contract then modifies state variables, creating opportunity for recursive callback exploitation

vulnerability_patterns.name: Cross-Function Reentrancy
vulnerability_patterns.description: Reentrancy across multiple functions sharing state
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Implement function-level reentrancy guards and state locking
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use nonReentrant modifier on all functions sharing state", "Implement mutex locks"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Multiple functions share state variables and at least one makes external call before updating shared state

vulnerability_patterns.name: Read-Only Reentrancy
vulnerability_patterns.description: View/pure function reads inconsistent state during reentrancy
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Use reentrancy guards even on view functions that read critical state
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Add nonReentrant to view functions", "Cache state before external calls"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: View or pure function reads state that can be manipulated during reentrancy attack on related contract

vulnerability_patterns.name: Missing Access Control
vulnerability_patterns.description: Critical function lacks access control modifiers allowing unauthorized execution
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Add onlyOwner, onlyRole, or custom access control modifiers
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use OpenZeppelin Ownable", "Implement AccessControl with roles", "Add require(msg.sender == owner)"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Function that modifies critical state or transfers value has no sender verification

vulnerability_patterns.name: Centralization Risk
vulnerability_patterns.description: Single owner has excessive control over contract operations
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Implement multi-sig, timelock, or decentralized governance
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use Gnosis Safe multisig", "Implement timelock delays", "Add governance voting"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Owner address has unilateral control over critical functions without multi-party approval or time delays

vulnerability_patterns.name: Privilege Escalation
vulnerability_patterns.description: User can escalate their privileges to admin/owner
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Ensure role assignment is properly protected and immutable where needed
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Protect role grant functions", "Use two-step ownership transfer"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Non-privileged user can call functions to grant themselves elevated permissions

vulnerability_patterns.name: Integer Overflow
vulnerability_patterns.description: Arithmetic operation can overflow beyond maximum value
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Use SafeMath library or Solidity 0.8+ built-in overflow protection
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use SafeMath.add()", "Upgrade to Solidity ^0.8.0", "Add overflow checks"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Addition, multiplication, or exponentiation without overflow protection in Solidity < 0.8.0

vulnerability_patterns.name: Integer Underflow
vulnerability_patterns.description: Arithmetic operation can underflow below zero
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Use SafeMath library or Solidity 0.8+ built-in underflow protection
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use SafeMath.sub()", "Add require checks", "Use checked arithmetic"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Subtraction without underflow protection allowing values to wrap around to maximum uint

vulnerability_patterns.name: Division by Zero
vulnerability_patterns.description: Division operation can be executed with zero denominator
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Add require statement to check denominator is not zero
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["require(denominator != 0, 'Division by zero')", "Use SafeMath.div()"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Division or modulo operation where denominator can be zero causing transaction revert

vulnerability_patterns.name: Unchecked External Call
vulnerability_patterns.description: Low-level call return value not checked for failure
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Check return value of call/delegatecall/staticcall
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["(bool success,) = addr.call(); require(success)", "Use high-level calls"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Low-level call, delegatecall, or staticcall with unchecked boolean return value

vulnerability_patterns.name: Unchecked Send/Transfer
vulnerability_patterns.description: Send return value not checked, transfer may fail silently
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Check return value of send or use transfer
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["require(addr.send(amount))", "Use addr.transfer(amount)"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Send operation return value ignored, potentially losing funds if transfer fails

vulnerability_patterns.name: Denial of Service - Unbounded Loop
vulnerability_patterns.description: Loop over unbounded array can exceed block gas limit
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Limit array size, use pagination, or redesign to avoid loops
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Implement max array size", "Use mapping instead of array", "Add pagination"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: For loop iterating over storage array with no size limit that could grow unbounded

vulnerability_patterns.name: Denial of Service - Block Gas Limit
vulnerability_patterns.description: Operation can be blocked by manipulating gas costs
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Avoid operations dependent on number of participants
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use pull over push payment pattern", "Implement circuit breaker"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Function performs operations proportional to user-controllable parameter that can exceed block gas limit

vulnerability_patterns.name: Denial of Service - Revert on Failure
vulnerability_patterns.description: External call failure causes entire batch operation to revert
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Handle failures gracefully, use pull payment pattern
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Try-catch external calls", "Skip failed transfers", "Pull payment pattern"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Loop making external calls where single failure reverts entire transaction affecting all participants

vulnerability_patterns.name: Block Timestamp Manipulation
vulnerability_patterns.description: Critical logic depends on block.timestamp which miners can manipulate
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Use block.number for time-dependent logic or accept timestamp tolerance
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use block.number instead", "Allow timestamp tolerance window"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Contract uses block.timestamp in critical comparison that miners can manipulate within ~900 second window

vulnerability_patterns.name: Transaction Order Dependence
vulnerability_patterns.description: Contract behavior depends on transaction ordering (front-running)
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Use commit-reveal scheme or implement order-independent logic
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Implement commit-reveal", "Use submarine sends", "Add slippage protection"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: State changes create value opportunity for observers who can execute transactions before target transaction

vulnerability_patterns.name: Weak Randomness
vulnerability_patterns.description: Pseudorandom number generated from predictable blockchain data
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Use Chainlink VRF or similar oracle-based randomness
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Implement Chainlink VRF", "Use commit-reveal with user entropy"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Random value derived from block.timestamp, block.difficulty, blockhash or other miner-influenced values

vulnerability_patterns.name: Delegatecall to Untrusted Contract
vulnerability_patterns.description: Delegatecall executes code in context of calling contract with arbitrary target
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Only delegatecall to trusted, immutable library addresses
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Hardcode library addresses", "Use library keyword", "Whitelist allowed targets"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Delegatecall target address comes from user input or mutable storage allowing arbitrary code execution

vulnerability_patterns.name: Storage Collision in Proxy
vulnerability_patterns.description: Proxy and implementation storage layouts conflict
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Use unstructured storage pattern or EIP-1967 standard slots
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Follow EIP-1967", "Use OpenZeppelin upgradeable contracts", "Implement unstructured storage"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Proxy contract and implementation use overlapping storage slots causing state corruption

vulnerability_patterns.name: Signature Replay Attack
vulnerability_patterns.description: Signed message can be reused across chains or transactions
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Include nonce, chainId, and contract address in signed message
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Add nonce tracking", "Include block.chainid", "Use EIP-712 structured data"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Signature verification lacks nonce, chainId, or contract address allowing message replay

vulnerability_patterns.name: Missing Signature Verification
vulnerability_patterns.description: Function accepts signed data without verifying signature
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Implement ecrecover signature verification
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use ecrecover", "Verify signer address", "Implement EIP-712"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Function processes signed parameters without recovering and validating signer address

vulnerability_patterns.name: Signature Malleability
vulnerability_patterns.description: Signature can be modified to different valid signature for same message
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Check signature s value is in lower half of curve
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Require s <= secp256k1n/2", "Use OpenZeppelin ECDSA library"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Ecrecover usage without checking s parameter allowing signature malleability

vulnerability_patterns.name: Uninitialized Storage Pointer
vulnerability_patterns.description: Storage pointer not initialized, points to slot 0
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Initialize storage variables or use memory keyword
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Add memory keyword", "Initialize before use", "Use Solidity 0.5+"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Local struct or array variable without memory/storage keyword defaults to storage slot 0

vulnerability_patterns.name: Uninitialized Proxy Implementation
vulnerability_patterns.description: Proxy implementation not initialized allowing takeover
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Initialize implementation in constructor or with initializer
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Call initialize() in constructor", "Use initializer modifier", "Lock implementation"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Upgradeable contract implementation has uninitialized state allowing anyone to initialize and take control

vulnerability_patterns.name: Unprotected Function Visibility
vulnerability_patterns.description: Critical function has public visibility instead of private/internal
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Set appropriate visibility (private/internal/external)
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Change to private/internal", "Add access control modifier"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Internal function or helper accidentally exposed as public allowing unauthorized access

vulnerability_patterns.name: Unprotected Constructor
vulnerability_patterns.description: Constructor is public allowing anyone to reinitialize
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Ensure constructor is only callable once during deployment
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use constructor keyword", "Upgrade to Solidity 0.5+"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Function with same name as contract but not using constructor keyword can be called by anyone

vulnerability_patterns.name: Selfdestruct to Arbitrary Address
vulnerability_patterns.description: Contract can be destroyed with funds sent to attacker address
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Remove selfdestruct or hardcode recipient address with access control
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Remove selfdestruct", "Hardcode recipient", "Add onlyOwner modifier"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Selfdestruct with user-controlled destination address allowing fund theft and contract destruction

vulnerability_patterns.name: External Contract Reference
vulnerability_patterns.description: Contract calls external address from user input
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Whitelist allowed external contracts
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Hardcode external addresses", "Maintain whitelist", "Validate contract code"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Contract makes call to address provided by user without validation

vulnerability_patterns.name: Gas Limit in External Call
vulnerability_patterns.description: External call forwards all available gas
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Specify gas limit for external calls
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Use call{gas: amount}", "Limit gas forwarded"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Call forwards all remaining gas to external contract increasing reentrancy risk

vulnerability_patterns.name: Constructor State Change
vulnerability_patterns.description: Constructor makes external calls before state is fully initialized
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Complete state initialization before external calls
vulnerability_patterns.fix_examples: ["Initialize all state first", "Move external calls to separate function"]
vulnerability_patterns.semantic_description: Constructor calls external contracts before finishing state initialization creating reentrancy risk

vulnerability_patterns.name: Locked Ether in Contract
vulnerability_patterns.description: Contract can receive Ether but has no mechanism to withdraw it, potentially locking funds permanently
vulnerability_patterns.remediation: Add a withdraw function with proper access control to allow authorized users to retrieve locked funds

vulnerabilities.title: Reentrancy Eth
vulnerabilities.description:
Reentrancy in VulnerableBank.withdraw() (ReEntrancy Contract.sol#19-29):
	External calls:
	- (success,None) = msg.sender.call{value: amount}() (ReEntrancy Contract.sol#24)
	State variables written after the call(s):
	- balances[msg.sender] = 0 (ReEntrancy Contract.sol#28)
	VulnerableBank.balances (ReEntrancy Contract.sol#12) can be used in cross function reentrancies:
	- VulnerableBank.balances (ReEntrancy Contract.sol#12)
	- VulnerableBank.deposit() (ReEntrancy Contract.sol#14-16)
	- VulnerableBank.withdraw() (ReEntrancy Contract.sol#19-29)
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use the Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern. Move external calls to the end of the function after all state changes.
